# VERDICT DFIR

> VERDICT is a free, open-source (Apache-2.0) digital-forensics & incident-response (DFIR) agent that
> runs inside an MCP-capable LLM agent (Claude Code is the reference agent). Point it at supported
> evidence and it opens a Case, drives a typed read-only MCP tool surface, verifies every Finding
> against the cited evidence, and writes a signed, offline-verifiable Verdict plus an analyst report.

Load-bearing guarantees: evidence is read-only; the audit log is append-only and hash-chained; every
reportable Finding must cite a `tool_call_id` that the verifier re-runs and hash-compares; there is no
`execute_shell` tool — the narrow typed tool surface is the security model. Verdict words are strictly
scoped — `NO_EVIL` means "no reportable Finding in the artifacts actually examined," never a clean
bill of health for a whole environment.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): what VERDICT is, the core claims, and the one-shot run path.
- [INSTALL](INSTALL.md): prerequisites and the install path (`bash scripts/setup`).
- [QUICKSTART](QUICKSTART.md): run modes (local, SIFT, fleet, watch) and environment choices.

## Architecture and guarantees

- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): subsystems, trust boundaries, prompt-vs-architectural guardrails, audit-visible self-correction.
- [MCP servers and tools](docs/reference/mcp-and-tools.md): the typed read-only tool inventory.
- [Verdict semantics](docs/verdict-semantics.md): what SUSPICIOUS / INDETERMINATE / NO_EVIL do and do not mean.
- [Fact fidelity](docs/fact-fidelity.md): the deterministic entailment check — a Finding cannot assert a value absent from its cited evidence.
- [Cryptographic attestation](docs/cryptographic-attestation.md): custody, the signed manifest, and offline verification.

## Contributing

- [Contribution model](docs/contribution-model.md): branch model (`main` vs `develop`), the local-gate CI (no Actions runners required), and `git ship` releases.
- [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md): exact build / test / submit commands and the non-negotiable invariants.
- [Help wanted](docs/help-wanted.md): the open problems and where to plug in.
- [Operating contract for agents](CLAUDE.md): the rules an LLM agent must follow when changing this repo.
- [Agent surface map](AGENTS.md): package layout and what agents may and may not touch.

## Notes for LLM agents working in this repo

- Claude Code (or any MCP-capable agent) is the investigation engine; do not reintroduce a removed orchestrator (`graph.py`, `api.py`, `cli.py`, `supervisor.py`, `specialists/`) or add `langgraph` / `fastapi` to the agent loop.
- Do not treat limited coverage as "clean", "cleared", or "no compromise"; report only what was actually parsed, with a cited `tool_call_id`.
- To contribute code: fork, branch off `develop`, run `bash scripts/run-all-smokes.sh` plus the per-language suites locally, then open a pull request against `develop` (never `main`).
